meta_description: Xometry offers the highest standard of precision among surface grinding operations. Surface grinding is a precision machining process that very precisely removes small amounts of material from the surface of a workpiece.

sections.heading: Surface Grinding Service by Xometry
sections.text: Surface Grinding Service by Xometry High-quality Surface Grinding Services in Days Not Weeks | Free Standard Shipping on All US Orders | Subject to availability. Xometry doesn't guarantee that we can provide this service at any given time.

sections.heading: What Is Surface Grinding?
sections.text: What Is Surface Grinding? Surface grinding is an aggressive and highly controlled interaction between a rotating grinding wheel and the workpiece. The grinding wheel, typically composed of abrasive particles of aluminum oxide, silicon carbide, or carborundum, is mounted on a spindle and rotates at high speeds. The workpiece is securely held on a magnetic chuck or a clamping fixture and is fed against the rotating grinding wheel. The wheel is generally mounted on a horizontal axis so its dressed cutting face can be lowered onto the workpieceâs surface. You may also find vertically mounted wheels meant to grind side faces, but theyâre less common.Â Most grinders mount the grinding wheel o...

sections.heading: Types of Materials for Surface Grinding
sections.text: Types of Materials for Surface Grinding Listed below are some of the materials that accept surface grinding: Aluminum Brass Cast Iron Mild Steel Stainless Steel Plastic Titanium

sections.heading: Aluminum
sections.text: Aluminum Surface grinding is not widely employed on aluminum parts. If the âgroundâ surface finish is desired, this metal usually responds better to an abrasive particle-coated fabric belt, creating a so-called brushed aluminum surface. There are, however, ways to avoid the spalling that often mars ground aluminum surfaces. Some useful tips are: Use a porous, open-structured silicon carbide wheel.Â Dress the wheel freshly and be prepared to re-dress it if the work is extensive. Forestall clogging by rubbing the wheel surface with wax. This will fill in the pores with non-abrasive material rather than allowing them to collect metal shavings and cause spalling.Â Run the wheel at high spee...

sections.heading: Advantages of Surface Grinding
sections.text: Advantages of Surface Grinding Surface grinding offers several benefits across the manufacturing sector: Environment-Friendly High Accuracy Smooth Finish Tight Tolerances

sections.heading: Environment-Friendly
sections.text: Environment-Friendly Surface grinding, by nature, generates little in the way of pollution. The cutting swarf is essentially inert and washed off the part by coolant. Itâs then filtered out and disposed of carefully. Energy requirements are lower than for some machining types. The machines rarely give off fumes and are even relatively quiet.Â High Accuracy Surface grinding can be used to trim parts to extremely precise dimensions. One approach starts by removing half of the planned material in the first pass, followed by half of the remainder, etc. Each pass removes half as much as the one before until the difference is so small as to be negligible. It creates an extremely precise toleran...

sections.heading: Disadvantages of Surface Grinding
sections.text: Disadvantages of Surface Grinding Listed below are some of the disadvantages of surface grinding: Costly Surface Contaminants Local Heat Effects

sections.heading: Costly
sections.text: Costly Surface grinding equipment is very precise and therefore costly. Surface Contaminants Residual abrasives embedded in the surface can disrupt the formation of a protective oxide film in stainless steel. This can invite corrosion when equipment is exposed to aggressive environments. These residual contaminants make surface grinding unacceptable as a finishing process for pharmaceutical processing equipment, among other sectors.Â Local Heat Effects Poor coolant supply and/or excessive cut depth can heat the workpiece enough to disrupt previous hardening effects. In the case of slides and plane bearings, that will compromise their resiliency.

sections.heading: Surface Contaminants

sections.heading: Alternatives to Surface Grinding
sections.text: Alternatives to Surface Grinding Xometry offers several alternative finishing options to surface grinding, though theyâre typically not as precise:Â Electropolishing Linishing As-Machined Grit Blasting Lapping/Honing/Scraping
